title: Dozens of Amendments Proposed for U.S. Crypto Market Structure Bill
summary: The Clarity Act, a major crypto bill, faces over 50 amendments ahead of a Senate hearing. These changes could significantly alter its impact on crypto regulation in the U.S. The bill's future remains uncertain as some lawmakers question its viability.
body:
The Clarity Act, a comprehensive bill aimed at regulating the U.S. crypto market, is now facing a wave of amendments as its Senate hearing approaches. Over 50 proposed changes could reshape key parts of the bill, including provisions related to securities classification, stablecoin regulation, and compliance requirements for crypto exchanges. These amendments reflect the diverse opinions among lawmakers and industry stakeholders about how crypto should be regulated.

The proposed changes include adjustments to the definition of securities, which could clarify which digital assets fall under the jurisdiction of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Additionally, there are amendments aimed at strengthening stablecoin oversight and ensuring that crypto exchanges adhere to stricter compliance standards. The bill's future is uncertain, as some lawmakers have expressed doubts about its ability to pass in its current form.

For everyday crypto users, the outcome of these amendments could have significant implications. Clearer regulations could provide more stability and confidence in the crypto market, potentially attracting more investment and innovation. However, stricter regulations could also impose additional compliance burdens on crypto businesses, which might affect the availability and cost of crypto services for consumers.

The Senate hearing on the Clarity Act is scheduled for next week, and the fate of the bill will largely depend on the outcome of these amendments. Crypto enthusiasts and industry participants should closely monitor the developments, as the final version of the bill could shape the regulatory landscape for years to come. Read more → https://www.coindesk.com/policy/2026/05/13/clarity-act-amendments-would-remake-key-parts-of-crypto-bill-but-have-doubtful-future
